articles.title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
articles.description:
"Jaylen Brown has been so resolute and dependable in the absence of Jayson Tatum and Boston is in second place in the East."

The post ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: ESPN hosts argue Jaylen Brown deserves NBA MVP for his performance.
Brown's contributions have kept the Celtics in second place in the East.
His potential MVP win could boost Boston's sports morale and fan engagement.

articles.title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
articles.description:
The boy was last seen Tuesday evening near Moraine Street wearing a black sweatshirt and black sweatpants, according to police.

The post Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Marshfield police are searching for an 8-year-old boy missing since Tuesday evening.
The boy was last seen near Moraine Street wearing a black sweatshirt and sweatpants.
Residents are urged to report any sightings to assist in the search efforts.

articles.title: At least 1,000 US troops from 82nd Airborne set to deploy to Mideast, AP sources say
articles.description:
It’s the latest addition of American troops to the Iran war effort after US officials recently said thousands of Marines aboard several Navy ships will be heading to the region.

The post At least 1,000 US troops from 82nd Airborne set to deploy to Mideast, AP sources say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: At least 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne are deploying to the Mideast.
This deployment adds to thousands of Marines already heading to the region.
Boston families may face uncertainty as local service members are involved in the Iran conflict.

articles.title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
articles.description:
"It'd be Marco's decision in terms of who goes in and who comes out of the lineup."

summary: Don Sweeney confirms lineup decisions rest with coach Marco.
James Hagens' potential playtime remains uncertain for the Bruins this season.
Local fans await clarity on Hagens' role as Bruins push for playoffs.

articles.title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
articles.description:
The case, which went cold for decades, centered around the killing of a former Mass. State Police colonel’s sister.

The post Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Man sentenced to life for 1986 murder of teenager in Plymouth forest.
Victim was sister of former Massachusetts State Police colonel.
Case closure brings justice after 40 years for Boston area residents.

articles.title: OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns
articles.description:
OpenAI said in a brief social media message Tuesday that it was “saying goodbye to the Sora app” and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

The post OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: OpenAI announced the discontinuation of the Sora app on March 24, 2026.
Users will soon receive guidance on preserving their created content.
Boston residents concerned about deepfakes face reduced access to AI video tools.

articles.title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
articles.description:
The pedestrian was crossing I-90 East when they were hit by a vehicle, police said.

The post Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A pedestrian was struck and killed on I-90 East in Boston.
The incident occurred on March 24, 2026, according to police reports.
Local authorities are investigating to enhance pedestrian safety measures on highways.

articles.title: Jayson Tatum’s son, Deuce, wrote a letter in Slam Magazine about Celtics star’s comeback
articles.description:
The eight-year-old wrote about the ups and downs of watching his dad deal with a significant injury.

The post Jayson Tatum’s son, Deuce, wrote a letter in Slam Magazine about Celtics star’s comeback appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Deuce Tatum, 8, wrote a letter in Slam Magazine about his father's injury struggles.
Jayson Tatum is making a comeback for the Boston Celtics after a significant injury.
The letter highlights the emotional journey of a young fan and his connection to the team.

articles.title: 5 teens arrested after Panera brawl; BPD has responded to location more than 15 times this year, department says
articles.description:
Five teenagers, aged 14 through 17 were arrested after allegedly kicking and throwing a table at police inside the Dorchester restaurant, Boston police said.

The post 5 teens arrested after Panera brawl; BPD has responded to location more than 15 times this year, department says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Five teens, aged 14 to 17, arrested for attacking police at Panera in Dorchester.
Boston Police have responded to this Panera location over 15 times in 2023.
Increased police presence may affect community safety and local business operations.
